title: Executive Order No. 88, s. 2025
text:
MALACANAN PALACE
MANILA

BY THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES

EXECUTIVE ORDER NO. 88

STRENGTHENING THE NATIONAL TASK FORCE DIWALWAL

WHEREAS, under Section 2, Article XII of the Constitution, the exploration,
development, and utilization of natural resources shall be under the full control and
supervision of the State;

WHEREAS, Proclamation No. 297 (s. 2002) declares the Diwalwal Mining
Reservation Area (DMRA), an area consisting of 8,100 hectares located in the
Municipality of Monkayo, Province of Davao de Oro, as a mineral reservation and an
environmentally critical area, and provides that mining operations in the DMRA may
only be undertaken either by the Department of Environment and Natural Resources
(DENR) or through qualified contractors authorized by DENR and the State;

WHEREAS, pursuant to Proclamation No. 297, the DENR designated the
Philippine Mining Development Corporation (PMDC), a government-owned
or -controlled corporation (GOCC), as its implementing arm in undertaking the mining
and mineral processing operations in the DMRA;

WHEREAS, Executive Order (EO) No. 217 (s. 2003), as amended by EO No.
258 (s. 2003), created the National Task Force (“Task Force”) Diwalwal to address
concerns regarding the significant ecological degradation in the DMRA caused by
unauthorized mining activities in the area;

WHEREAS, it has been observed that unauthorized mining activities have
resumed in the DMRA, making it imperative to strengthen Task Force Diwalwal to
ensure that concerns regarding illegal mining activities, environmental degradation,
and socio-economic development in the DMRA are properly addressed; and

WHEREAS, Section 17, Article VII of the Constitution vests in the President the
power of control over all Executive departments, bureaus, and offices, and the
mandate to ensure the faithful execution of laws;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, FERDINAND R. MARCOS, JR., President of the

Philippines, by virtue of the powers vested in me by the Constitution and existing laws,
do hereby order:

THE PRESIDENT OF THE PHILIPPINES
 
Section 1. Reconstitution of National Task Force Diwalwal. The National

Task Force Diwalwal is hereby reconstituted. The Task Force shall be composed of

the following:
Chairperson : Secretary, DENR
Members : - Representative, Office of the President;

Secretary, Department of National Defense;
Secretary, Department of the Interior and Local Government; and
Chairperson, Mindanao Development Authority.

The Chairperson and members of the Task Force may designate their

respective alternates, with ranks not lower than Assistant Secretary or its equivalent,
who shall be fully authorized to decide for or on their behalf.

Section 2. Duties and Functions. The Task Force shall have the primary duty

of overseeing all mining activities in the DMRA, with the end in view of creating an
environment conducive to lasting peace and sustainable development in the DMRA.
For this purpose, the Task Force shall perform the following duties and functions,
subject to existing laws, rules and regulations:

a.

Oversee the implementation of all government programs, activities and projects
for the rehabilitation and development of the DMRA;

Operationalize and coordinate effective and timely execution of measures to
regulate and rationalize all mining activities in the DMRA. For this purpose, the
Task Force shall conduct an inventory and evaluation of all mining activities and
tenements in the DMRA, particularly those entered into and/or under the
management of PMDC. In this regard, the Task Force shall oversee and monitor
PMDC’s mining activities in the DMRA;

Study and implement measures and ways forward, subject to applicable laws,
rules and regulations, to address all issues and concerns affecting the
rehabilitation and development of the DMRA. This may include, among others: (i)
formulating a comprehensive plan which shall serve as the strategic framework to
promote environmental and ecological protection of the area and raise awareness
among stakeholders regarding issues and concerns in the DMRA, as well as the
efforts of the government to address the same; (ii) as may be necessary,
transferring the remaining mining tenements in DMRA, if any, from PMDC to the
Natural Resources and Development Corporation; and (iii) issuing the necessary
implementing guidelines for the effective implementation of this Order;

Invite, collaborate, and establish institutional partnerships with concerned
government agencies and the private sector, including non-government
organizations and other stakeholders, to ensure the effective performance of its
duties and functions;

As may be necessary, create, convene, and reorganize committees or technical
working groups to assist in the implementation of this Order;
 
f. Submit to the Office of the President, through the Office of the Executive
Secretary, annual reports on the implementation of this Order; and

g. Perform such other functions as may be directed by the President and/or the
Executive Secretary.

Section 3. Convergence. All concerned government agencies are hereby
directed, and all local government units within the Province of Davao de Oro and the
private sector are hereby encouraged, to render full support to and cooperation with
the Task Force to ensure the effective implementation of this Order.

Section 4. Funding. The funding requirements for the initial implementation of
this Order shall be charged against the existing appropriations of the
member-agencies of the Task Force, subject to relevant budgeting, accounting, and
auditing laws, rules and regulations. Thereafter, the subsequent funding requirements
for the purpose shall be included in the budget proposals of said member-agencies,
subject to the usual budget preparation process.

Section 5. Separability. If any part or provision of this Order is held invalid or
unconstitutional, the provisions not affected thereby shall remain in full force and
effect.

Section 6. Repeal. All issuances, orders, rules and regulations, or parts
thereof, that are inconsistent with this Order, are hereby repealed or modified
accordingly.

Section 7. Effectivity. This Order shall take effect immediately upon its
publication in the Official Gazette or a newspaper of general circulation.

DONE, in the City of Manila, this 4th — dayof July _ in the
year of Our Lord, Two Thousand and Twenty-Five.

 

By the President:
